body.summary: LOCK 015 is safe for local execution provided it follows the established v0.1 metadata-proof pattern. The governance spine (LOCKs 002-014) is complete and ready for a meta-readiness layer. Key risk: ensure the 'governanceReadiness' scope is locked to v0.1 governance spine only (no database/API/cloud readiness checks).

review_items.description: LOCK 015 occupies a natural position after the golden scenario regression pack (LOCK 014). The governance spine (classifier → router → guard → trace → pipeline → review gate → work order → prompt composer → dry run → golden scenarios) is a complete chain. A meta-readiness layer that inspects this chain without executing it is architecturally sound.
review_items.suggestion: Proceed as planned. LOCK 015 is the meta-capstone of v0.1.

review_items.description: If LOCK 015 follows the v0.1 pattern (pure read-only computation, no side effects), it is safe to run on this machine. Every existing module (escalationGuard, policyEngine, governancePipeline, etc.) is metadata-proof: they compute classifications and decisions but never execute agents, call models, or mutate state.
review_items.suggestion: Mirror the GOVERNANCE_DRY_RUN_CONSTANTS pattern: include a boundary warning constant and assert execution_allowed=false, dispatch_allowed=false, mutation_allowed=false, persistence_allowed=false in the output.

review_items.description: The spec says LOCK 015 'reports readiness for future runtime/provider/dashboard layers.' This is a scope-landmine. If generateGovernanceReadinessSummary attempts to check for the existence of Node.js HTTP servers, installed provider SDKs, database drivers, or dashboard frameworks, it would require filesystem scanning or process inspection — both of which expand the attack surface.
review_items.suggestion: Restrict the readiness check to the v0.1 governance spine only. Check that all LOCK modules exist, export the expected functions, and that configs/schemas are valid. Do NOT check for runtime infrastructure. If future readiness must be reported, return a static 'NOT_YET_BUILT' for those domains. No fs.readdir, no require.resolve(), no process inspection beyond what validate-config.js already does.

review_items.description: LOCK 015 must not import or invoke any HTTP client, API wrapper, or model SDK. The v0.1 scope explicitly prohibits live model calls, cloud exposure, and OpenRouter/OpenAI/Anthropic integration. Any 'readiness' check that pings an API endpoint is a scope violation.
review_items.suggestion: Ban imports of http, https, fetch, node-fetch, openai, @anthropic-ai/sdk, openrouter, or any other network-capable module. Import audit should be part of LOCK 015 validation.

review_items.description: LOCK 015 must not write to any database (SQLite, JSON file, or otherwise) or modify any config files. The v0.1 project scope explicitly lists 'No database persistence' as out-of-scope. Even a 'readiness_check_log.json' would violate the boundary.
review_items.suggestion: Output must be ephemeral (return-only, no disk writes). If a Markdown report is generated (per LOCK report pattern), it should follow the existing report convention in reports/LOCK-015-*.md and be the ONLY file write.

review_items.description: The existing modules (governanceDryRun, taskClassifier, etc.) all include robust null/malformed input handling. LOCK 015 must do the same. A readiness summary function that crashes on null input could be called unsafely.
review_items.suggestion: Include input normalization similar to normalizeDryRunInput. Gracefully handle null, undefined, non-object, and malformed inputs. Return a safe default (NOT_YET_READY or UNKNOWN) rather than throwing.

review_items.description: All existing module output constants are Object.freeze()'d. LOCK 015 should follow this pattern to prevent accidental mutation of readiness status objects that are passed downstream.
review_items.suggestion: Freeze the readiness status constants (GOVERNANCE_SPINE_READY, etc.) with Object.freeze(). Return deeply frozen or new objects from the function.

review_items.description: LOCK 015 must not add new top-level folders, database schemas, API route definitions, or dashboard components. The v0.1 file layout (src/core/, configs/, governance/, docs/, reports/, tests/) is locked.
review_items.suggestion: New file should be src/core/governanceReadiness/index.js only. Test file at tests/unit/governanceReadiness.test.js. Report at reports/LOCK-015-governance-readiness-summary-REPORT.md. No other file changes.

review_items.description: Validation must include the standard LOCK validation suite plus a drift scan for forbidden patterns (runtime, provider, persistence, mutation). The LOCK-014 report explicitly recommends proceeding to LOCK 015 'only after operator review.'
review_items.suggestion: Run: npm run validate, npm test, node --check on new source, JSON parse check on any fixtures, drift scan for forbidden imports/patterns. Include boundary assertion tests (execution_allowed=false, etc.) in the unit tests.
